events.event: George I ascends to the throne, beginning the Hanoverian dynasty in Great Britain.
The Slavery Abolition Act 1833 comes into effect, abolishing slavery throughout most of the British Empire.
Joseph Priestley discovers oxygen, a major breakthrough in British chemistry.
King William II (William Rufus) is killed by an arrow while hunting in the New Forest.
Iraq invades Kuwait, leading to the Gulf War in which British forces played a major role.
The birth of James Robertson Justice, the celebrated British character actor.
Sir Edward Grey delivers his 'lamps are going out' speech as the UK nears entry into WWI.
The death of Joseph Conrad, the influential Polish-British novelist.
James II of Scotland is killed by a bursting cannon at the Siege of Roxburgh.
The United Kingdom declares war on Germany following the invasion of Belgium.
The birth of Queen Elizabeth The Queen Mother at Belgravia, London.
The birth of Percy Bysshe Shelley, the major English Romantic poet.
The Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty is signed by the UK, USA, and USSR.
The first transatlantic telegraph cable is completed, connecting the UK and North America.
The birth of Ray Clemence, the legendary England and Liverpool goalkeeper.
The birth of Alfred Lord Tennyson, the Poet Laureate of Great Britain during the Victorian era.
The atomic bombing of Hiroshima; the event was a turning point for British post-war policy.
The birth of Andy Warhol; his pop art heavily influenced the UK art scene in the 1960s.
The first British Grand Prix is held at the Brooklands circuit in Surrey.
The birth of Bruce Dickinson, the lead singer of the British heavy metal band Iron Maiden.
The birth of Tobin Bell; the actor's work has been a staple of UK horror cinema fans.
The Great Train Robbery takes place at Bridego Railway Bridge in Buckinghamshire.
The Battle of Gravelines ends with the Spanish Armada in full retreat.
The birth of Roger Federer; his dominance at Wimbledon is a significant part of UK sporting history.
The coronation of King Edward VII and Queen Alexandra takes place at Westminster Abbey.
The birth of Sam Elliott; the American actor's work is widely popular in the UK.
The birth of Izaak Walton, the author of 'The Compleat Angler'.
King Charles II lays the foundation stone of the Royal Observatory, Greenwich.
The first ever night of the Proms (The Promenade Concerts) takes place in London.
The birth of Antonio Banderas; the actor has been a prominent figure in UK cinema.
The death of Andrew Carnegie, the Scottish-American industrialist and philanthropist.
A total solar eclipse is visible across parts of the UK, the first since 1927.
The birth of Enid Blyton, the prolific English children's author.
The death of William Blake, the visionary English poet and artist.
The death of Ian Fleming, the creator of James Bond.
The birth of Mark Knopfler, the lead singer and guitarist of Dire Straits.
The Battle of Blenheim: The Duke of Marlborough leads Allied forces to victory over the French.
The birth of Alfred Hitchcock, the legendary English film director, in London.
The death of Florence Nightingale, the 'Lady with the Lamp'.
The Atlantic Charter is issued by Winston Churchill and Franklin D. Roosevelt.
VJ Day (Victory over Japan Day) is announced, marking the end of WWII.
The death of Bertolt Brecht; his work had a profound impact on British theatre.
King Macbeth of Scotland is killed at the Battle of Lumphanan.
The birth of Sir Walter Scott, the major Scottish novelist and poet.
India and Pakistan gain independence from British rule.
The Peterloo Massacre: Cavalry charge a crowd of 60,000 protesters in Manchester.
The death of Elvis Presley; his cultural impact in the UK was monumental.
The birth of James Cameron; the filmmaker's work is a mainstay of UK cinema.
The Omagh bombing, the deadliest attack of 'The Troubles', kills 29 people.
The birth of Robert De Niro; his films have been highly influential on British actors.
The first English settlers arrive at Roanoke Island in North America.
The death of Waties Waring; though American, his civil rights work was followed in the UK.
The birth of Roman Polanski; his work in the UK film industry was significant.
The birth of Robert Redford; his films are a staple of UK popular culture.

Village Hall Development: Significant early records for the management of the Biddenham Village Hall are formalised. The hall, situated opposite the Three Tuns, remains the heart of the village's social and voluntary activities.
Mary, Queen of Scots, returns to Scotland from France to begin her reign.
The death of James Watt, the Scottish engineer and inventor (New Style date).
The birth of Bill Clinton; the President's visits to the UK were major political events.
Winston Churchill delivers his 'Never was so much owed by so many to so few' speech.
The birth of David Walliams, the English comedian, actor, and children's author.
Ronald Ross discovers the malaria parasite, a major achievement in British tropical medicine.
The birth of Usain Bolt; his Olympic success in London 2012 is a legendary sporting moment.
The birth of Joe Strummer, the lead singer and guitarist of The Clash.
The birth of William IV, the last monarch of the House of Hanover.
The Battle of Bosworth Field: Henry VII defeats Richard III, ending the Wars of the Roses.
The death of Michael Collins, the Irish leader, during the Irish Civil War.
The birth of Claude Debussy; his music is a fixture in UK classical programming.
The execution of William Wallace at Smithfield in London.
The death of Rudolph Valentino; the silent film star was immensely popular in the UK.
The birth of Keith Moon, the legendary drummer of The Who.
British troops burn the White House and other government buildings in Washington D.C.
Matthew Webb becomes the first person to swim across the English Channel.
The birth of Stephen Fry, the acclaimed English actor, writer, and broadcaster.
The death of James Watt, the Scottish engineer and inventor (Old Style date).
The birth of Sean Connery, the legendary Scottish actor and the first cinematic James Bond.
The first ever daily international air service begins between London and Paris.
The Battle of Crécy: English forces under Edward III defeat the French.
The birth of Mother Teresa; her work with UK charities was significant.
The birth of James Corden, the English actor, comedian, and television host.
Lord Mountbatten is killed by an IRA bomb on his boat in Sligo.
The Anglo-Zanzibar War takes place, the shortest war in history (38 minutes).
The birth of Joan Bennett; the actress was a familiar face in UK cinemas.
The Slavery Abolition Act 1833 receives Royal Assent.
The March on Washington; the civil rights movement was closely followed in the UK.
The birth of Michael Winner, the English film director and restaurant critic.
The Beatles play their last ever live concert at Candlestick Park in San Francisco.
The 'Ashes' are born as Australia beats England at cricket for the first time on English soil.
The birth of Michael Jackson; his concerts in the UK were legendary cultural events.
The birth of Fred MacMurray; the actor was a staple of UK television matinees.
The birth of Cameron Diaz; her films have been consistently popular in the UK.
The birth of Ernest Rutherford, the Nobel Prize-winning physicist based in the UK.
Diana, Princess of Wales, dies in a car crash in Paris.
The death of King Henry V in France.
The birth of Van Morrison, the influential Northern Irish singer-songwriter.
